meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve tutorial hell (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing to overcome tutorial hell. A beginner-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to master your first project.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop watching and start shipping. By building a functional SaaS pricing model—integrating Stripe, managing tiers, and handling webhooks—you transform passive consumption into a tangible asset. This checklist uses Antigravity and Claude Code to bridge the gap between theory and a deployable, revenue-ready application.
content.intro:
You have spent countless hours watching polished tutorials, yet your cursor blinks on a blank screen. You are trapped in the 'tutorial hell' loop, where you understand the code while watching, but lose your agency the moment the video ends. The solution isn't another course; it is the friction of real-world constraints.

Building a SaaS pricing module forces you to confront the architecture of value. It moves you from 'writing code' to 'engineering a business.' By integrating real payment logic, you transition from student to builder, transforming your portfolio from a collection of exercises into a functional, professional-grade SaaS application.

steps.title: Define Your Tier Architecture
steps.description: Establish a clear pricing strategy: Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers. This forces you to map out feature gating in your database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema for a SaaS application including User, Subscription, and Plan models with support for three distinct pricing tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create models for User and Subscription tiers using Prisma'

steps.title: Stripe Integration Setup
steps.description: Connect your backend to Stripe. This is where you learn to handle secret keys and API lifecycle management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust service module to handle Stripe session creation for recurring monthly subscriptions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'install stripe and initialize the nodejs client with environment variables'

steps.title: Webhook Event Orchestration
steps.description: Implement webhooks to sync Stripe's status with your local database. This is the heartbeat of your SaaS app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure express route to handle Stripe webhooks, specifically listening for 'checkout.session.completed' to update user subscription status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add a webhook listener route that verifies stripe signatures'

steps.title: Frontend Pricing Table Component
steps.description: Build a responsive UI that dynamically renders your pricing tiers based on your backend configuration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React pricing component using Tailwind CSS that accepts a data array of plan objects and displays them with a toggle for monthly/yearly billing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a pricing table component using tailwind css'

steps.title: Deployment and Verification
steps.description: Deploy your app to a live environment and verify the end-to-end flow using Stripe's test mode.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for testing Stripe integration in a staging environment to ensure all edge cases for subscription cancellation are handled.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'run automated tests to verify the checkout flow'

pro_tips: Always build your database schema before writing a single line of UI code; data structure dictates your application's limits.
Use Stripe CLI locally during development to pipe events directly to your machine, bypassing the need for public tunnels.
Never hardcode your pricing IDs; store them in environment variables to easily switch between test and production keys.
Implement 'graceful degradation'—if the payment service is down, ensure users can still access free features without throwing 500 errors.
Focus on the 'Happy Path' first: just make one payment work before worrying about complex edge cases like subscription upgrades or downgrades.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing model is live, integrate a 'usage-based' billing trigger to move from flat-rate pricing to a high-value enterprise model.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for the sake of learning and start building for the sake of shipping. You have the tools, the architecture, and the roadmap. The only thing missing is your execution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Connect your backend to Stripe. This is where you learn to handle secret keys and API lifecycle management.
Implement webhooks to sync Stripe's status with your local database. This is the heartbeat of your SaaS app.
